data.title: Lululemon Athletica gains after issuing positive guidance for the holiday quarter
data.description:
Lululemon Athletica (LULU) provided an update on Monday ahead of the athletic apparel company's appearance at the ICR Conference. Lululemon (LULU) guided for Q4 revenue and earnings per share to be toward the high end of the previously guided ranges of $3.500B to $3.585B and $4.66 to $4.76, respectively. There was no change to the previous guidance covering gross margin, selling, general and administrative expenses, or the effective tax rate.

"Based on our performance over the holiday period, we expect to achieve the higher end of our previously communicated fourth quarter net revenue and EPS guidance. We remain focused on executing our action plan to drive improvement in our U.S. business and look forward to the opportunities in front of us," highlighted CFO Meghan Frank.

Lululemon (LULU) started the year with pressure on the board from Elliott Investment Management and founder Dennis Wilson to make dramatic changes. In the C-suite, current CEO Calvin McDonald is scheduled to step down from his role on January 31. Following McDonald’s departure, CFO Meghan Frank and Chief Commercial Officer André Maestrini will serve as interim co-CEOs until a full-time replacement is named.

Shares of Lululemon (LULU) rose 2.2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Savers Value Village guides Q4 and FY25 revenue above consensus
data.description:
• Savers Value Village (SVV) reported total net sales of $464.7 million, up 15.6% year over year, beating the consensus estimate of $458.63 million. U.S. sales grew 20.6%, while net sales in Canada increased 3.1%.
  • Comparable store sales rose 5.4% overall, led by an 8.8% increase in the U.S. and a 0.7% increase in Canada.
  • For fiscal 2025, net sales increased 9.2% to $1.68 billion, slightly above expectations of $1.67 billion. U.S. net sales rose 12.9%, while Canadian net sales increased 3.6%.
  • Total comparable store sales for the year rose 4.7%, with the U.S. up 6.6% and Canada up 2.0%.
  • The company reaffirmed its fiscal 2025 adjusted net income outlook of approximately $71 million to $75 million, or $0.44 to $0.46 per diluted share, and its adjusted EBITDA outlook of approximately $252 million to $257 million. Consensus earnings expectations stand at $0.45 per share.
  • The company will meet with investors and deliver a presentation at 8:00 a.m. Eastern Standard Time on Monday, January 12, 2026, at the 28th Annual ICR Conference.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank partners with T. Rowe Price across the GCC
data.description:
• First Abu Dhabi Bank ((FAB)) has entered into a strategic partnership with T. Rowe Price (TROW), under which T. Rowe will serve as FAB’s investment partner, offering solutions across equities, fixed income, alternatives, and multi-asset strategies throughout the GCC region.
  • The deal broadens FAB’s investment offerings and strengthens its overall investment management proposition.
  • FAB managed AED 1.38T ($376B) in assets as of September 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cracker Barrel provides an investor update ahead of its ICR Conference appearance
data.description:
Cracker Barrel Old Country Store (CBRL) issued an investor presentation ahead of the company's appearance at the ICR Conference this week,

As of fiscal 2025, Cracker Barrel noted that it operated 656 company-owned stores across 43 states, employed approximately 77,000 people, generated $3.48 billion in total revenue, and derived about 19% of total sales from retail and about 20% of restaurant sales from off-premise channels, underscoring the importance of both its retail component and to-go business. Management described a multi-pronged effort to return performance to a positive trajectory, centered on evolving operations (including back-of-house optimization, training/retraining, and key leadership changes), reconnecting with guests through menu, marketing, and value initiatives, and tightening cost and cash management via corporate restructuring, reduced advertising spend, and lower capital expenditures, all anchored by a focus on food and the guest experience. 

In the SEC filing, Cracker Barrel (CBRL) pointed to its rewards program as a key value and engagement vehicle, with membership expanding 10.7 million on a Q1 FY26 year-to-date basis from 1.4 million in Q1 FY24 to 10.7 million in Q2 FY26.

On the balance sheet, the company ended Q1 FY26 with $550.3 million of total debt and $485 million of available liquidity, reclassified its primary covenant in December 2025 from a consolidated total leverage ratio to a consolidated senior leverage ratio that excludes convertible debt (standing at 0.3x versus a 3.0x maximum), Cracker Barrel (CBRL) also highlighted its plan to use its revolver to retire the remaining $149.4 million of 2026 convertible notes closer to the June 2026 maturity, alongside longer-dated 2030 converts and $65 million outstanding on the revolver.

Shares of Cracker Barrel (CBRL) are up 18.2% for the early part of 2026. Short interest stands at 22.9% of the total float.

data.title: Five stocks to watch at the biggest retail conference of the year
data.description:
The three-day ICR Conference will be held from January 12–14 in Orlando, Florida. The conference is considered one of the most important in the retail sector due to the large number of holiday sales reports and guidance updates from the management teams from over 250 public and private growth companies.

The agenda includes formal presentations, fireside chats, and small-group discussions where management teams discuss strategy, financial outlook, growth initiatives, and capital allocation, alongside topical sessions on areas such as shareholder activism, M&A, AI’s impact on consumer and retail, sports-related investment themes, and IPO readiness. 

Five notable companies that analysts have circled this year as having the potential to see share price gains after their presentations and updates include Five Below (FIVE), On Holdings (ONON), Planet Fitness (PLNT), Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF), and SharkNinja (SN). The expectation is that positive pre-announcements on Q4 results could spark rallies.

Walmart (WMT), Nike (NKE), and Lululemon (LULU) will also be making important ICR appearances.

The retail sector as a whole has a track record of outperforming the broad market during the ICR Conference.

data.title: Odds of Netflix closing WBD deal holding steady despite Trump opposition
data.description:
Odds of Netflix (NFLX) acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) held steady on prediction markets even as President Donald Trump signaled his disapproval about the deal. 

On Kalshi, the chances of WBD going with the streaming giant stood at 54%. On Polymarket, the odds were at 53%. 

Trump reposted on Truth Social an opinion piece from attorney John Pierce titled "Stop the Netflix Cultural Takeover," which was published in One America News last month.

While Warner Bros.' (WBD) board approved Netflix's (NFLX) $83B offer, it rebuffed Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) $108B hostile bid. Paramount's CEO David Ellison, as well as his father Larry, is a Trump ally.

Trump last month said the Netflix-WBD deal "could be a problem," saying Netflix (NFLX) has "a very big market share… and when they have Warner Bros., that share goes up a lot."

data.title: QXO boosts Apollo-led financing by $1.8B
data.description:
QXO (QXO) has announced a $1.8B increase to its previously announced $1.2 billion financing led by funds managed by affiliates of Apollo Global Management (APO), bringing the total investment in the distribution firm to $3B.

Apollo, Temasek, and certain other investors have agreed to make the investment through the previously disclosed series of convertible perpetual preferred stock. The investment further strengthens QXO’s financial flexibility to pursue strategic acquisition opportunities.

Under the investment agreement, the investors have committed to purchase Series C Preferred Stock to fund one or more qualifying acquisitions through July 15, 2026.

This commitment will be extended for up to an additional 12 months if a definitive acquisition agreement is executed before the initial commitment period expires. Any issuance of the Series C Preferred Stock will close at or around the closing of the qualifying acquisition(s).

Source: Press Release

data.title: Natera expects a 35% jump in FY revenue
AbbVie to buy Arizona manufacturing facility from West Pharmaceutical
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) has agreed to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona and associated intellectual property from West Pharmaceutical Services (WST).

West Pharmaceutical is selling all manufacturing and supply rights for SmartDose 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System and associated facilities to AbbVie (ABBV) for total consideration of $112.5M at close, subject to working capital and other adjustments.

The transaction is anticipated to close in mid-2026 and will significantly expand AbbVie's drug delivery device manufacturing capabilities and capacity.

AbbVie plans to hire around 200 employees at the site and invest more than $175M to acquire, as well as modernize and fully integrate it into its global manufacturing network.

The acquisition marks progress against AbbVie (ABBV)'s previously announced commitment to invest more than $10B of capital in the U.S. over the next decade to broadly support innovation and expand critical manufacturing capabilities and capacity.

SmartDose 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System revenues are anticipated to be around 4% of revenues in fiscal year 2025, West Pharmaceutical Services said in a separate news release. The company expects to provide additional details when it gives 2026 guidance on its fourth quarter 2025 earnings call in February.

Source: Press Release

data.title: BE Semiconductor shares rise as Q4 orders surge
data.description:
• Shares of BE Semiconductor Industries N.V. (BESIY) climbed on Monday after the company reported fourth-quarter orders of about €250 million, up 43% from the prior quarter and more than doubling from a year earlier.
  • For the second half of 2025, orders were approximately € 425 million, an increase of 63% versus the first half of 2025. 
  • Order strength in Q4-25 versus Q3-25 was principally due to a broad-based increase in bookings by Asian subcontractors for 2.5D data center applications and renewed capacity purchases by leading photonics customers, the company said.
  • The increase also reflected the receipt of anticipated hybrid bonding orders during the quarter.
  • BESI, which designs and manufactures semiconductor equipment, opened almost 8% higher on Monday in Amsterdam.
